domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical home activities for Noah, focusing on movement and physical skills appropriate for his developmental level. Each activity is designed to support his physical development while ensuring safety and comfort.
We do not think Noah has a meaningful delay in Social / Emotional and may not need special support in this category right now.
This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Noah's language and communication development at home, focusing on vocalization, turn-taking, and gestures.
This activity bank provides practical home activities for Noah to support cognitive and adaptive skill development, focusing on attention, problem-solving, and exploration.

activities.title: Tummy Time with Arm Support
activities.description: Place Noah on his tummy on a soft surface. Use a rolled towel or small pillow under his arms to help him push up onto his elbows. Encourage him to look around and reach for toys placed just out of reach. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires.

activities.title: Sound Imitation
activities.description: Make different sounds like 'ma', 'ba', and 'da' and encourage Noah to imitate you. Use a mirror to make it more engaging. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires.

activities.title: Supported Sitting Practice
activities.description: Sit Noah on the floor with pillows or cushions around him for support. Encourage him to lean forward and reach for toys placed in front of him. Provide gentle support to help him maintain balance. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires.

activities.title: Turn-Taking Sounds
activities.description: Sit face-to-face with Noah and take turns making sounds. Start with a sound and wait for him to respond before making another sound. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires.

activities.title: Toy Grasping and Bringing to Mouth
activities.description: Give Noah a soft toy that is easy to grasp. Encourage him to bring it to his mouth. You can help guide his hand if needed. This promotes fine motor skills and hand-mouth coordination. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Keep the child upright and closely supervised. Avoid choking-risk foods and stop if coughing, gagging, or distress occurs.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Noah. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

This activity bank provides practical language and communication activities for Maya, focusing on enhancing her expressive and receptive language skills through engaging, short tasks suitable for home.
This activity bank provides a variety of cognitive and adaptive activities for Maya, focusing on skill-building through play and exploration.

activities.title: Toy Naming Game
activities.description: Hold up a familiar toy and say its name clearly. Encourage Maya to look at the toy when you say its name. Repeat with different toys.

activities.title: Block Stacking
activities.description: Encourage Maya to stack two small blocks. You can model the activity by stacking the blocks yourself first, then let her try. Celebrate her efforts!

activities.title: Follow the Direction
activities.description: Give Maya a simple one-step direction like 'give me the toy' without gestures. Praise her when she follows the direction.

activities.title: Chore Imitation
activities.description: Model a simple chore like sweeping with a broom. Hand her a small broom or a toy broom and encourage her to copy your actions.

activities.title: Point and Ask
activities.description: Place a few toys within reach. Encourage Maya to point to a toy she wants and say 'that one' or another simple word. Respond to her request.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Maya. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides a variety of movement and physical activities tailored for Leo, focusing on developing his motor skills in a safe and engaging way.
This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Leo's social and emotional development, focusing on engagement, imitation, and emotional connection.

This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Leo's language and communication development at home. Each activity focuses on enhancing communication skills through engaging and manageable tasks.

This activity bank provides a variety of short, practical activities to support Leo's cognitive and adaptive skills. Each activity is designed to be engaging, easy to start, and suitable for home practice.

activities.title: Follow the Gesture
activities.description: Hold out a toy and say, 'Give me the toy.' Encourage Leo to hand it to you. Use gestures to guide him if needed. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Container Play
activities.description: Provide a container and various small items (like blocks or toys). Encourage Leo to hold the container with one hand while using the other hand to take items in and out. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Point and Ask
activities.description: Place two toys in front of Leo. Encourage him to point to the one he wants while saying a word like 'car' or 'ball.' Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Joint Attention with Toys
activities.description: Play with a toy that makes noise or has lights. Show Leo how it works, then encourage him to show you the toy when he plays with it. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Name That Object
activities.description: Show Leo a familiar object and say its name. Encourage him to look at it when you name it. Repeat with different objects. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides a variety of movement and physical activities tailored for Sofia, focusing on enhancing her gross motor skills and self-help motor skills. Each activity is designed to be practical for home use and appropriate for her developmental level.
This activity bank includes practical home activities designed to support Sofia's social and emotional development, focusing on engagement and interaction.
This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Sofia's language and communication skills, focusing on expressive language and comprehension.
This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Sofia's cognitive and adaptive skills, focusing on following directions, problem-solving, and functional play.

activities.title: Ball Kicking Fun
activities.description: Set up a soft ball in a clear space. Encourage Sofia to kick the ball with her foot. You can roll the ball back to her to keep the game going.

activities.title: Clean-Up Time Together
activities.description: At clean-up time, say 'It's clean-up time!' and encourage Sofia to help pick up toys. Show her how to do it and praise her efforts.

activities.title: Spoon Practice
activities.description: Provide a bowl of soft food (like yogurt or applesauce) and a spoon. Encourage Sofia to scoop the food and bring it to her mouth, offering help as needed.

activities.title: Look at Me Game
activities.description: Take turns showing each other something fun you can do, like a silly face or dance move. Encourage Sofia to say 'Look at me!' when it’s her turn.

activities.title: Stair Climbing
activities.description: Find a set of stairs. With support, help Sofia walk up a few steps, holding her hand for balance. Encourage her to place one foot on each step.

activities.title: Clothing Practice
activities.description: Provide Ethan with loose clothing items. Encourage him to practice taking off and putting on these items while seated, offering assistance as needed. Use stable support and close adult supervision throughout.

activities.title: Color Sorting
activities.description: Gather a few colored items (like blocks or toys). Ask Ethan to sort them by color into different containers. You can help him name the colors as he sorts.

activities.title: Book Page Turning
activities.description: Sit with Ethan and read a book together. Encourage him to turn the pages one at a time, providing hand-over-hand support if necessary. Use stable support and close adult supervision throughout.

activities.title: Counting Steps
activities.description: While standing at the bottom of a set of stairs, practice counting the steps as Ethan takes them one at a time with support. Encourage him to count aloud.

activities.title: Supported Step-Ups
activities.description: Using a low step or sturdy box, help Ethan practice stepping up and down while holding onto a stable surface for support. Focus on one foot at a time. Use stable support and close adult supervision throughout.

This activity bank provides a variety of short, practical activities designed to support Amina's cognitive and adaptive skills, focusing on attention, problem-solving, and following directions.

activities.title: Color Sorting
activities.description: Gather a few colored items (like blocks or crayons). Ask Amina to sort them by color. For example, say, 'Can you put the red ones here and the blue ones there?' Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Ball Catching Practice
activities.description: Sit on the floor with Amina and roll a large, soft ball back and forth. Encourage her to catch the ball when you roll it to her. Celebrate her successes! Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Two-Step Instructions
activities.description: Give Amina simple two-step instructions, such as 'Pick up the toy and put it in the box.' Celebrate her success to encourage her. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Crayon Drawing
activities.description: Provide Amina with crayons and paper. Encourage her to hold the crayon between her fingers and thumb to make marks on the paper. You can draw simple shapes for her to copy. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Pretend Play with Dolls
activities.description: Use dolls or stuffed animals to engage in pretend play. For example, feed the doll and encourage Amina to do the same. Ask her questions like, 'What does the doll want to eat?' Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical home activities for Lucas, focusing on movement and physical skills suitable for his developmental level. Each activity is designed to be short, manageable, and supportive of his needs.

This activity bank provides a variety of short, practical activities to support Lucas's social and emotional development at home. Each activity focuses on enhancing social engagement and emotional regulation through interactive play.

This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Lucas's language and communication development at home. Each activity is designed to be engaging and suitable for his current developmental level while promoting early vocalization, turn-taking, and receptive language skills.
This activity bank provides a variety of cognitive and adaptive activities tailored for Lucas, focusing on skill-building in a safe and engaging manner.

activities.title: Sound Imitation Game
activities.description: Make simple sounds like 'ah', 'oo', or 'ee' and encourage Lucas to imitate you. Use a mirror to make it more engaging. Repeat the sounds together. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Supported Sitting Practice
activities.description: Place Lucas in a sitting position on a soft surface with cushions around him for support. Encourage him to reach for toys placed just out of reach to promote balance and posture. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Turn-Taking Sounds
activities.description: Sit facing Lucas and take turns making sounds. Start with a sound and wait for him to respond. Encourage him to make a sound back to you. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Crawling to Toys
activities.description: Place toys a short distance away from Lucas while he is on his tummy. Encourage him to crawl towards the toys, providing support as needed. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Follow the Sound
activities.description: Make a sound (like clapping or a gentle noise) and encourage Lucas to turn his head towards you. Praise him when he does. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Lucas. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

This activity bank includes short, practical activities designed to support Zain's cognitive and adaptive skills while considering his attention and self-regulation needs.

activities.title: Emotion Charades
activities.description: Take turns acting out different emotions using facial expressions and body language. The other person guesses the emotion. Start with simple emotions like happy, sad, or surprised. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Color Hunt
activities.description: Ask Zain to find and bring you three items of a specific color around the house. Start with one color and gradually add more as he gets comfortable. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Helping Hands
activities.description: Choose a simple household task (like sorting laundry or setting the table) and invite Zain to help. Encourage him to talk about what he is doing and how it helps. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Story Sequence Cards
activities.description: Use simple picture cards from a familiar story. Show Zain the cards and ask him to put them in order. Discuss what happens next in the story. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Story Time with Feelings
activities.description: Read a short story together and pause to discuss the characters' feelings. Ask Zain how he thinks the characters feel and why. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Social / Emotional, Cognitive / Adaptive for Zain. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank includes practical home activities designed to support Isla's movement and physical skills, focusing on posture, strength, balance, and coordination. Each activity is tailored to her developmental level and safety needs.

This activity bank includes six home activities designed to support Isla's social and emotional development, focusing on emotional regulation and peer interaction. Each activity is practical, safe, and tailored to her current developmental level, with a mix of current-level and near next-step practices.

This activity bank provides practical, home-based activities to support Isla's language and communication development. Each activity is designed to be engaging, safe, and appropriate for her developmental level.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities for Isla to support her cognitive and adaptive skills, focusing on following directions, problem-solving, and imitation play. Each activity is designed to be short, safe, and suitable for home practice.

activities.title: Spoon Feeding Practice
activities.description: Provide Isla with a bowl of soft food and a spoon. Encourage her to scoop the food and bring it to her mouth. Offer hand-over-hand support if needed. Use stable support and close adult supervision throughout. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Color Sorting
activities.description: Gather a few colored objects (like blocks or crayons). Ask Isla to sort them by color into different containers. For example, say, 'Can you put the red ones here and the blue ones there?' Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Ball Kicking Fun
activities.description: Roll a soft ball towards Isla and encourage her to kick it back to you. Use a large, soft ball to make it easier for her to connect. Use stable support and close adult supervision throughout. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Two-Step Instructions
activities.description: Give Isla two simple instructions to follow, such as 'Pick up the toy and put it on the table.' Praise her when she completes both steps. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Walking Up Stairs with Help
activities.description: Find a few stairs at home. Hold Isla's hand and help her step up one or two stairs at a time, encouraging her to lift her feet. Use stable support and close adult supervision throughout. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Isla. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Luna's social and emotional development, focusing on engagement and emotional regulation.

This activity bank provides practical and engaging activities to support Luna's language and communication skills at home. Each activity is designed to be short and manageable for parents while focusing on enhancing Luna's expressive and receptive language abilities.

This activity bank provides a variety of cognitive and adaptive activities for Luna, focusing on skill-building in a fun and engaging way. Each activity is designed to be short and practical for home use, with a mix of current-level and near next-step practices.

activities.title: Body Part Pointing
activities.description: Ask Luna to point to different body parts on herself or on a doll. Start with simple requests like 'Where's your nose?' and gradually include more parts.

activities.title: Emotion Faces Game
activities.description: Make different facial expressions (happy, sad, surprised) and encourage Luna to imitate them. Take turns making faces and guessing each other's emotions.

activities.title: Two-Word Requests
activities.description: Practice using two-word phrases by modeling examples like 'more juice' or 'big truck'. Encourage Luna to repeat and use similar phrases when asking for things.

activities.title: Turn-Taking with Toys
activities.description: Play with a favorite toy that requires taking turns (like a ball or a puzzle). Encourage Luna to wait for her turn and celebrate when she does.

activities.title: Gesture Game
activities.description: Play a game where you model different gestures (like blowing a kiss or nodding). Encourage Luna to imitate the gestures, praising her efforts.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Social / Emotional, Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Luna. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

activities.title: Transition Countdown
activities.description: Before transitioning to a new activity, give Ryan a countdown (e.g., 'In 3 minutes, we will clean up'). Use a timer if possible to visually show the countdown. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Emotion Charades
activities.description: Take turns making faces to show different emotions (happy, sad, surprised). Ryan can imitate your expressions, and you can discuss what makes you feel that way. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Helping Hands
activities.description: Choose a simple task (like sorting toys or setting the table) and invite Ryan to help. Praise him for his efforts and discuss how helping makes you both feel. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Story Time with Feelings
activities.description: Read a short story together. Pause to discuss the characters' feelings and ask Ryan how he would feel in similar situations. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Pretend Play Scenarios
activities.description: Engage in a short pretend play scenario where Ryan can be a teacher or superhero. Encourage him to interact with you in character. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Social / Emotional for Ryan. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.
